name: future_tense_rejection
description: Pins the FUTURE_TENSE_COMPLIANCE rejection (engine/validator.py:413-427) AND the retry-exhaustion → abstain path (assessor.py loop at :843, max_retries=2 → 3 attempts). Each queued proposal is status=Compliant with a narrative that classifies as COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING (contains 'confirmed via' / 'configured per' / 'examined ') AND matches _FUTURE_TENSE_RE ('will be configured'). Status/narrative-class check passes, but the future-tense trip-wire fires every attempt. After 3 rejections the orchestrator calls _abstain with reason 'validator-exhausted: ...'. This is the precision-over-recall mechanism the priorities memo ranks as #2 — a planned-state narrative must never be allowed to ship as Compliant. Three identical proposals (not three variants) because the stub does not consume corrective_context; the orchestrator's job is to keep retrying until the budget is gone, and the case pins that contract.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization establishes and documents configuration settings for information technology products employed within the information system.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine documented configuration settings and confirm baseline values.

tagged_evidence:

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined USD00088888 Example System Configuration Management Plan Rev A; confirmed via section 4 that all baseline settings will be configured per the documented values.
